heading: Introduction
text: SeqUIaSCOPE open-source web application designed routine clinical oncology diagnostics patient-centric integration visualization annotated genomic variants, fusion events, expression profiles. includes embedded genome browsing, pathway-level interpretation, customizable reporting. patient-centric design supports single-patient diagnosis allowing multi-patient data management. SeqUIaSCOPE designed secure local cluster-based deployment using Docker Kubernetes, ensuring patient data remains within institutional infrastructure.

heading: Live demo
text:
hosting live demo SeqUIaSCOPE showcase capabilities allow users explore platform action.
Access demo : Live DemoImportant Notes:Demo data anonymized represent real clinical casesSome features may limited functionality due data sensitivity requirementsGenome browsing available fusion gene dataset, limited one specific fusion (KMT2A::MLLT3) immediate vicinity breakpointsWhile demo runs full version SeqUIaScope, uploading data supportedFor production use data, deploy SeqUIaSCOPE locally using Docker cluster using Helm chart

heading: How to load demo data:
text: Select demo_data folder directory patient dataAdd patient name DZ1601Select datasets (Somatic variant calling, Germline variant calling, Fusion gene detection Expression profile).Leave patterns somatic germline variants empty.Fill fusion detection pattern tumor BAM file fusion chimeric BAM file chimeric. Genome IGV snapshot set GRCh38/hg38Fill reference tissues expression profile spleen, testis

heading: How to install and run
text: SeqUIaSCOPE can deployed two ways:cluster using Kubernetes + Helm — suitable shared production environmentsLocally using Docker Compose — suitable single-machine usePre-built images scenarios available Docker Hub — need build anything :

heading: Option A — Cluster deployment (Kubernetes + Helm)
text: cluster environments, SeqUIaSCOPE can deployed using provided Helm chart. Full instructions configuration options maintained separate repository:👉 sequiascope-helmThe Helm chart uses pre-built Docker Hub images listed .

heading: Prerequisites
text: Docker ≥ 24Docker Compose (already installed)

heading: 2. Download the compose file:
text: application uses two separate volumes:input_files: Read-bind mount input dataoutput_files: Writable bind mount session output data

heading: 3. Pull and start
code:
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d

heading: 4. Open in browser
text: app may take 10–20 seconds initialize first launch.

heading: IGV snapshots
text: sequiascope-igv container generates IGV screenshots fusion gene visualizations BAM files provided. watches output_files/sessions/ processes batch files written app.

heading: Configuration
text: app reads reference_paths.json working directory. default config embedded image. override , mount :

heading: Build from source
text: Building source required standard use. need modify application :
code:
git clone https://github.com/katjur01/seqUIaSCOPE.git
cd seqUIaSCOPE
docker compose build
docker compose up -d

heading: Troubleshooting
text: App doesn’t startNo data visible uploadCheck file names match expected patterns (see Data requirements)Verify mount: docker exec sequiascope-app ls /input_filesIGV snapshots generatedCheck IGV container running: docker ps | grep igvCheck IGV logs: docker compose logs igv

heading: Notes
text: input_files directory mounted read-IGV server read-write SeqUIaSCOPEBoth services communicate via internal Docker network — manual network configuration neededSession data stored within containers; use Docker volumes persistence neededAll data remains local machine; nothing sent external serversFor organizational deployment: ’re interested using SeqUIaSCOPE don’t Docker expertise, contact bioinformatics team department. can help set maintain SeqUIaSCOPE organization’s infrastructure.

heading: Data Requirements
text:
SeqUIaSCOPE accepts four types genomic data: somatic variant calling results,
germline variant calling results, fusion gene detection results, gene expression profiles.
four required — can upload combination depending analysis needs.uploading, make sure files follow naming format rules described page.
application automatically scans directory files uses rules identify
file belongs patient dataset type. Errors upload time almost always
caused naming issues.

heading: Input Files Overview
text:
Tip: choice, always prefer .tsv .xlsx. TSV files load faster less likely cause formatting issues.Fusion gene detection requires results Arriba STARFusion upstream tools.
required input file pre-processed table combining output callers.
Arriba .pdf .tsv output files separate optional — unlock expanded row preview Fusion Genes module.

heading: File Naming Rules
text:
SeqUIaSCOPE identifies files scanning full file path (folder names + file name together).
looks specific keywords anywhere path decide type file .
means keyword can appear filename parent folder name — work.

heading: Keyword Reference
text:
keyword can live anywhere path — two examples work:Patient ID path: Every per-patient file must patient ID somewhere full path
(either filename parent folder). Files without patient ID matched.
TMB file (mutation_loads) Genes Interest file exceptions —
shared across patients need patient ID path.
code:
project_root/patient_001/somatic_variants.tsv    # keyword in filename
project_root/somatic_data/patient_001/variants.tsv  # keyword in folder name

heading: BAM Files
text:
BAM files don’t use keyword system. Instead, define patterns upload
(e.g. tumor, FFPE, chimeric) application finds BAM files whose names match patterns.
See Upload Data — Step 1 details.every .bam file, corresponding index file must exist directory,
named either file.bam.bai file.bai.

heading: Arriba Output Files
text:
provide Arriba output files, .pdf .tsv must present matched pair
(base name, different extension). .pdf without matching .tsv ignored, vice versa.

heading: Expression Files with Multiple Tissues
text:
comparing multiple reference tissues, provide one file per tissue.
tissue name must appear filename (e.g. blood_expression.tsv, liver.tsv).
Use underscores instead spaces — blood_vessel.tsv, blood vessel.tsv.Tissue names enter upload form must match names filenames.
tissue names provided, application looks single expression file per patient
containing expression RNAseq path.

heading: Directory Layouts
text:
SeqUIaSCOPE flexible — data can organised many different ways long naming
rules respected. three common layouts work correctly.
key principle patient ID dataset keyword must appear
somewhere file’s path.Choose root directory carefully. upload form select single root directory
contains patient data. Pick specific folder still contains everything —
selecting broad directory (e.g. home folder) may cause scanner pick 
unrelated files confuse file matching.Option — one folder per data typeBest pipeline already separates outputs analysis type.Option B — BAM files stored separately analysis resultsCommon primary (alignment) secondary (variant/fusion calling) outputs live separate trees.Option C — files flat per patientWorks smaller projects single pipeline writes everything one folder per patient.

selecting data directory, specifying patients datasets, validating required
files found analysis begins.previously saved session, can skip process entirely loading directly
— see Session Management .

opens /input_files default — directory mounted application
running via Docker. running application locally without Docker, opens
working directory.Choose specific directory still contains patient folders. Selecting 
-broad directory (e.g. home folder) may cause file scanner pick unrelated
files. See Data Requirements — Directory Layouts guidance.

multiple IDs , separated commas new lines. Patient IDs must match identifiers

tasks can take several minutes depending number fusions size BAM files.Crucially, runs background — need wait finish. can
immediately start exploring somatic variants, germline variants, expression data 
modules snapshot generation progress. Fusion Genes module progressively
update snapshots become available.application detects snapshots previous session already exist 
dataset, dialog ask whether want Resume (reuse existing snapshots 
generate missing ones) Clean Start (delete existing outputs start fresh). 
useful previous session interrupted partway .::: {.alert .alert-warning} selected Don’t create IGV snapshots Step 1, IGV
snapshot generation skipped Arriba report images still extracted Arriba files
available. expanded row preview Fusion Genes module contain Arriba
visualisations IGV images.

heading: Session Management
text:
SeqUIaSCOPE supports saving loading analysis sessions diagnostic work can 
interrupted resumed point without repeating upload validation steps.Saving session — click 💾 icon top navigation bar point 
analysis. session file captures current configuration, selected patients datasets,
flagged variants, fusions, genes, notes added. file saved 
output_files/ directory.Loading session — click Load session button top right corner Step 1.
Select previously saved session file. application restore configuration 
selections, skipping directory selection validation steps.Accidental navigation: try close refresh browser tab data 
loaded, application show warning asking confirm. prevent accidental
loss unsaved work.

data SeqUIaSCOPE — variants, fusions, expression results — displayed using
interactive tables built reactable package. page describes features
common tables. need read exploring modules,

first open module, table necessarily showing available data.
module applies set default filters load focus view 
clinically relevant entries keep application responsive large datasets.somatic germline variant calling, default filters restrictive —
see Variant Calling — Default Filters exact criteria.
fusion gene detection expression profile, default filters less aggressive
column selection filter menu may hide columns default.table appears empty unexpectedly small, check active filters assuming

heading: Sorting
text:
Click column header sort table column. Clicking reverses order.
third click removes sort.Multi-column sorting supported holding Shift clicking additional column
headers. sorts first primary column, additional column order
clicked . remove secondary sort, hold Shift click column header 
cycles back unsorted.

heading: Filtering and Column Visibility
text:
column filter input directly header. Typing filter box narrows
table rows matching input column. Filters across multiple columns 
combined — row must satisfy active filters remain visible.filter menu (accessible via filter icon table) provides additional
filtering options specific module, well column visibility selector 
lets show hide individual columns focus matters analysis.Tip: Apply filters flagging variants fusions report. Working 
filtered subset significantly reduces table reload times keeps application responsive.

text:
Column widths can adjusted dragging border column headers left right.
available main data tables (variants, fusions, expression).

heading: Exporting Data
text:
tables can exported using download buttons table. Two export options
available:Export filtered — exports rows currently visible applying filtersExport — exports complete dataset regardless active filtersSupported export formats TSV, XLSX, CSV.

Visualises relationships somatic variants, associated genes,

heading: Expression Profile

Expression Profile module enables exploration gene expression changes across one 
reference tissues. provides two views: Genes Interest (available GOI

volcano plot.general information working tables (sorting, filtering, column visibility,

heading: The Expression Table
text:
Expression data displayed colour-coded rows highlight deregulated genes glance:Red — upregulated genes (log2FC > 1)Blue — downregulated genes (log2FC < -1)Orange — genes p-value < 0.05Green — genes p-adj < 0.05When multiple reference tissues available, tissue set log2FC,
p-value, p-adj columns, grouped tissue name.

removed column groups table.Pathways — filter rows pathway classification using searchable dropdown.Tissue values — tissue, apply quick preset filters: log2FC > 1,
log2FC < -1, p-value < 0.05, p-adj < 0.05. Multiple filters tissue
combined logic.Column visibility — show hide individual columns.

Click Load IGV Viewer initialise browser current tracks locus.

heading: Network Graph
text:
Network Graph module provides interactive visualisation protein-protein interactions
within biological pathways, contextualising molecular alterations identified previous modules.
network built using interaction data STRING DB pathway
annotations KEGG, rendered Cytoscape.js.module consists two interconnected views: main graph showing genes within 
selected pathway, subgraph showing focused selection nodes detailed analysis.

heading: Graph Elements
text:
Nodes represent genes. coloured log2FC value expression data:Red (increasing intensity) — upregulated genes (log2FC > 1)White — significant changeBlue (increasing intensity) — downregulated genes (log2FC < -1)Node borders coloured highlight variants fusions flagged previous modules:Toggle Add somatic variants, Add germline variants, Add fusions using 
switches main graph highlight corresponding genes distinctive border colours.Tip: Use variant highlighting quickly identify genes actionable mutations within
pathway contexts.Edges represent STRING DB protein-protein interactions. Two display modes available:Confidence (default) — one edge per gene pair, edge width reflecting combined
confidence scoreEvidence — multiple edges per gene pair, one type supporting evidence
(experiments, databases, textmining, coexpression, neighborhood, gene fusion, cooccurrence)

heading: Pathway and Layout
text:
Pathway — select KEGG pathway display. pathways relevant patient’s
expression data shown.Layout — choose cola (recommended smaller graphs, ~100 nodes) 
fcose (recommended large complex networks).Tissue — multiple reference tissues available, select tissue’s log2FC
values used node colouring.

heading: Edge Filtering
text:
Interaction sources — select types STRING evidence include network.
sources selected default.Interaction score — set minimum confidence threshold displayed edges. Available
levels: lowest confidence (0.15), medium (0.4, default), high (0.7), highest (0.9). Higher
thresholds produce fewer reliable interactions.Tip: Adjusting confidence threshold significantly changes network density. Start 
medium confidence (0.4) increase graph dense interpret.

heading: Graph Manipulation
text:
Three buttons available main graph:Fit graph — adjusts view fit nodes within visible area. nodes 
selected, fits selection instead.Clear selection — deselects selected nodes.Select first neighbors — selects nodes directly connected currently selected
node, useful exploring local network neighbourhood.Nodes can dragged reposition . Click node select ; click deselect.

shows selected nodes interactions isolated view.Additional genes can added subgraph entering gene names Add new genes
field, even present currently selected pathway. Nodes can removed
subgraph using Remove nodes field. main graph subgraph share
edge display settings confidence thresholds.Note: adding genes present original pathway, ensure gene names 
correctly formatted avoid unrecognised entries.

main graph subgraph associated tables showing gene name,
log2FC, p-value, pathway name, number pathways gene participates .
Clicking row table highlights corresponding node yellow graph.

Summary Report module provides consolidated overview findings flagged 
previous modules enables generation clinical report .docx format.

overview boxes, flagged variants, fusions, genes previous modules 
displayed expandable panels, one per dataset type.Somatic variants — panel header shows gene name consequence. Expanding reveals:Variant info: chromosomal position, Ref/Alt alleles, HGVSc, HGVSp, variant typeFrequency: allelic frequency (VAF), gnomAD NFE frequencyGermline variants — panel header shows gene name, consequence, ClinVar significance.
Expanding reveals:Variant info: chromosomal position, Ref/Alt alleles, HGVSc, HGVSp, variant typeFrequency: allelic frequency, gnomAD NFE frequencyFusion genes — panel header shows gene pair Arriba confidence level.
Expanding reveals:fusion partner: position Arriba site annotationReading frame (Arriba)Overall support (read coverage)Expression profile — expandable. Shows total number deregulated genes
selected report (combining flagged genes Genes Interest 
Genes tabs).

heading: Generating the Report
text:
Click download icon top right module open report options. Two
template options available:Default template — built-template configured via reference_paths.json
(see Configuration)Custom template — upload .docx file institutional formattingClick Generate Report download populated .docx file.automatically generated report regarded draft. requires manual
review completion clinician use clinical context.

heading: Custom Templates
text:
Custom templates use keyword-based variable substitution. Place keywords .docx
template want corresponding content appear — application replaces 
keyword relevant patient data generating report.keywords must wrapped double angle brackets exactly shown.keyword present template data available section (e.g. 
fusions flagged), placeholder replaced default message rather left
empty. Keywords present template simply ignored.Tip: Test template sample data using clinical workflow ensure
placeholders substituted correctly formatting preserved.

heading: Configuration
text:
SeqUIaSCOPE reads reference_paths.json file startup locate reference files 
configure optional features. default version file embedded Docker image.
need override defaults — example use custom report template
custom reference genome — mount reference_paths.json container.

heading: Mounting a Custom Config
text: Add following volume mount docker-compose.yaml:Place reference_paths.json directory compose file.

heading: output_dir
text:
Directory session data, IGV snapshots, Arriba reports stored. Defaults 
output_files. Docker deployments match writable volume mount.

heading: reference_files
text:
Paths three built-reference files. paths relative application
root inside container.

heading: custom_genome
text:
Optional section configuring custom reference genome IGV. Remove section
entirely using one built-genomes (GRCh38/hg38, GRCh37/hg19, etc.).Custom genome files must placed inside input_files directory 
accessible via /input_files mount. Paths config relative mount point.
